# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - develop
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main
      - develop

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5 # Fail fast for build and test

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Set up Node.js (example, adjust for your language)
      uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: '20' # Or your desired version

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: npm ci --prefer-offline --no-audit # Use npm ci for clean install, prefer-offline for speed

    - name: Run unit tests
      run: npm test -- --coverage=false # Or your test command, disable coverage for speed

    - name: Build application
      run: npm run build

  deploy-staging:
    needs: build-and-test # Only deploy if build and tests pass
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5 # Fail fast for deploy
    environment: staging # Define a staging environment for secrets/variables

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: Deploy to staging (example - replace with your actual deployment steps)
      run: |
        echo "Deploying to staging..."
        # Example: rsync, scp, AWS CLI, Azure CLI, GCP gcloud, Kubernetes apply, etc.
        # This is a placeholder. You'll need to replace this with your actual deployment logic.
        echo "Deployment script would go here, e.g., using SSH, cloud provider CLI, or a deployment tool."
        echo "Accessing staging secrets would use: ${{ secrets.STAGING_SERVER_HOST }}"
        # For demonstration:
        echo "Application deployed to staging successfully!"
